channel.title: Jornal TRIBUNA DE MACAU EN
channel.description: Jornal TRIBUNA DE MACAU EN RSS

item.title: Las Vegas casinos earn less than a third of Macau's in 2025
item.description:
default
Last year, Macau's gross gaming revenues were more than three times higher than the revenues of casinos on the Las Vegas strip. The US$8.8 billion raised by the North American gaming capital in 2025 was equivalent to 29% of the revenues of Macao SAR casinos, according to official data analyzed by the TRIBUNA DE MACAU newspaper. Despite having recorded gambling revenues similar to those of 2024, Las Vegas' tourism sector reached the lowest figures in the last four years, in terms of visitors, contrasting with Macau's record year
PEDRO MILHEIRÃO In 2025, the casinos on the Las Vegas strip collected gross revenues of 8.8 billion US dollars (71.3 billion patacas), a similar amount to that recorded in the previous year, according to data from the Nevada State Gaming Control Commission compiled by the newspaper TRIBUNA DE MACAU. This amount corresponded to 28.8% of Macau's gaming revenue last year, amounting to 247.4 billion patacas (9.1% more in annual terms), according to the Macau Gaming Inspection and Coordination Department. In the overall calculation of 2024, gambling in Las Vegas had reached revenues equivalent to 31.4% of the 226.8 billion achieved by Macau casinos, which shows that the difference between the two regions has increased. The State of Nevada, which encompasses Las Vegas, recorded just over half of Macau's revenues in 2025. Revenues of 15.8 billion US dollars (127.7 billion patacas) from casinos in the American state reflected an annual increase of 1.2% and corresponded to 51.6% of gaming results in the Macao SAR. In the year 2024, the Nevada gaming sector had recorded revenues of 15.6 billion US dollars (126.2 billion patacas), translating into a growth of 0.5% compared to 2023. However, this amount represented 55.6% of the revenue of Macau casinos in the same year. According to data analyzed by this newspaper, last year, the average daily gross gaming revenue in Macau was 677.8 million patacas. The monthly average of Las Vegas casinos was equivalent to 5.9 billion patacas, which means that the Macao SAR's gaming industry only needed nine days to reach the monthly earnings of the strip sector in the American city. At the end of December 2025, the Las Vegas strip had 58 casinos, practically triple those in Macau (20). It should be noted that the number of projects counted by the regulatory authorities of the State of Nevada undergoes monthly fluctuations. Nevada's monthly average was 10.6 billion patacas, meaning Macau only needed 16 days to beat the monthly results of the US state's casinos. Nevada's tallies included 444 casinos in December. Although the sector's growth stagnated last year, compared to 2019, a pre-pandemic year and during which Las Vegas achieved revenues of 6.6 billion US dollars, the North American city's gaming industry recorded growth of 33.8%. Similarly, the State of Nevada recorded 12 billion in gross revenue in 2019, having grown 31.3%, on the same comparative basis. Macau, in turn, achieved only 84.6% of the 2019 results (292.5 billion patacas) last year.
Minimum number of visitors in four years In contrast to the record year for Macau's tourism sector in 2025, Las Vegas recorded the lowest number of visitors in four years. The North American gaming capital received 38.5 million tourists last year, reflecting an annual drop of 7.5%, according to the Convention and Visitors Authority (LVCVA). The 43-day shutdown of the US Government and the drop in visitors from Canada, as a result of the boycott of the trade war and tariffs imposed by the Trump administration, are some of the factors that explain the decline in Las Vegas tourism, especially if we consider that, since 2022, Canadians constituted more than a quarter of international tourists at Harry Reid International Airport, with an increasing trend. In 2024, 1.44 million passengers at the airport came from Canada, equivalent to 29.2% of the 4.97 million international visitors. In the previous year, 1.39 million passengers had arrived from Canada, while in 2022 this number stood at 885.6 thousand. Analyzing LVCVA data, overall, the number of passengers at Harry Reid International Airport decreased by 5.9%, between 2024 and 2025, ending the last year with a total of 54.99 million people. Using the same comparative basis, the average price of a room on the Las Vegas strip fell from 193.13 to 183.54 US dollars (-4.5%) while the occupancy rate of the hotel sector in that area fell 3.2 percentage points to 83.2%. Last year, despite the international and leisure segments registering declines during the year, “convention attendance remained resilient”, says the LVCVA, taking into account that the sector received almost 6 million visitors, a figure similar to 2024. Steve Hill, chief executive of the organization, said that the year was one of the most complex operating environments that Las Vegas faced, excluding the pandemic, and that the city was considered “too expensive”, according to the publication “The Nevada Independent”.
Expected growth of up to 13% in the first quarter Steven Pizzella, an analyst at Deutsche Bank, predicted that Macau's gross gaming revenues would amount to US$8.1 billion in the first three months of this year, equivalent to 65.2 billion patacas. According to the banking institution, cited by the GGRAsia portal, this value would represent an annual growth of 11.7%. More optimistic, DS Kim, Selina Li and Lindsey Qian, analysts at JP Morgan, consider that Macau's casinos are on track to achieve an annual increase of 13%, in the first quarter of 2026. Vitaly Umansky, analyst at Seaport Research Partners, foresees a year-on-year increase of 13.4%, between January and March. It should be remembered that, according to the Gaming Inspection and Coordination Department, the gross revenues of the game amounted to MOP20.6 billion in February, corresponding to an annual growth of 4.5% and 81.3% of the value recorded in the same month of 2019, the pre-pandemic year. In the first two months of this year as a whole, gross gaming revenues increased by 13.9% to 43.26 billion patacas year-on-year, reaching 86% of the level accumulated in the same period of 2019. According to the JP Morgan team, in the last week of February, gross gaming revenues stood at a daily average of 1 billion patacas, compared to an average of 785 million during Chinese New Year week. The investment bank classified February's results as “impressive, taking into account the relatively smooth performance during the Lunar New Year holiday period, something that analysts attributed “to a timing dynamic” and not “to weak demand”. Novo Chinês, representing a daily average of 172,699 tourists and a growth of 5%. In the Macao SAR budget for 2026, the Government predicted gross revenue from the game of 236 billion patacas in the overall year.
Gross revenue in 2025 (in patacas) Macau: 247.4 billion Strip Las Vegas: 71.3 billion

item.title: Revitalization does not affect the structure of the Coloane Market
item.description:
The revitalization plan for the Coloane Market is not yet known in detail, but authorities have indicated that it will not affect the structure of the pavilion or the sloping roof. They also indicated that they intend to “introduce artistic and young elements”, improve the pedestrian environment and create an environment focused on restaurants and cultural and creative industries
Catarina Pereira
The revitalization plan for the Coloane Market, which is not yet known, intends to maintain the main structure of the space. “Based on the maintenance of the structure of the pavilion and the sloping roof, which are located in the middle of the architectural complex, the functional transformation and image renewal” of the market will be carried out, said the director of Tourism Services (DST), Helena de Senna Fernandes, citing information from the Institute for Municipal Affairs (IAM). As he said, in response to a question from deputy Loi I Weng, the idea will be to “introduce artistic and young elements, optimizing the pedestrian environment, creating an environment composed of ‘conservation + restoration + cultural and creative industries/retail + leisure’, in order to boost the flow of people and inject new vitality into the economy of that area”. Furthermore, Senna Fernandes indicated that IAM “is launching a public tender for the preparation of the work project”. Remember that the Coloane Market, the only one on the island and which began operating in 1893, closed its doors on December 16, 2024. The evolution of Coloane, the change in the town's demographic structure and the modification of residents' consumption habits were identified as factors that contributed to the continuous decrease in the flow of visitors to that space – and which led to its extinction. At the time, the IAM indicated that the revitalization of the original construction of the Coloane Market would be considered, and the proposal would later be presented to the public. So far, the detailed plan is still unknown. The newspaper TRIBUNA DE MACAU yesterday questioned the IAM about whether there is already a concrete plan, what it recommends and when it will be presented to the public. The organization only said that, at the moment, it has no information on the matter. During the interpellation, deputy Loi I Weng asked about the same matter, but received no response.
IC plans guided tours Loi I Weng also questioned the Government about whether it intends to create a system of interpretation and guided tours of heritage, as well as optimizing the pedestrian system and tourist circuits, to “integrate Coloane into a multifunctional and multilevel cultural agglomeration space, and an area of ​​historical experience”. In this context, the Cultural Institute (IC) begins by saying that it has been increasing resources relating to heritage on the island of Coloane, “through survey and classification processes”. “In accordance with the general revitalization plan promoted by the Macao SAR Government, it has also carried out the structured restoration of the Lai Chi Vun Naval Shipyards,” he added. On the other hand, he stated that he plans to launch “Guided cultural tours”, which will focus on themes such as the villages, customs, traditions and temples of Coloane. "With this initiative, the aim is to increase the cultural and tourist attractiveness of the island, leading participants to visit and get to know, in depth, the unique towns and customs of Coloane. Through these visits, a connection will be made between cultural heritage sites and the area's own architecture, with the aim of deepening the knowledge of residents and tourists", highlighted the organization. Loi I Weng also questioned whether the authorities intend to “take the lead” in creating “distinctive” thematic economic activities in Coloane. DST pointed out that it has been continuously developing “extensive promotional work”. Furthermore, through financial support programs, it continues to encourage local associations to take advantage of community and maritime resources, working together with neighborhood stores to organize characteristic tourist activities. According to Senna Fernandes, in 2026, six activities were financed to be carried out on the Coloane Pier Bridge and in the surrounding urban areas, covering various experiences such as guided tours, games and workshops, “revealing the humanistic history and community characteristics of Coloane, guiding visitors and residents to a more in-depth exploration, with a view to promoting community tourism and economic development”.

item.title: Project in Pátio do Amparo must include data on the former Chinese Customs House
item.description:
The urban conditions plan for a plot of land in Pátio do Amparo, where a Chinese Customs House was thought to have existed, is once again up for consultation, but after investigation by the Cultural Institute it was concluded that it was not in that location. Still, according to the document, the project should include an area to display historical information precisely about the Chinese Customs
Catarina Pereira
After advances and setbacks, the urban conditions plan (PCU) of a plot of land in Pátio do Amparo, specifically numbers 5 to 7, which falls within the protection zone of the Ruins of São Paulo, is for the third time being the subject of opinion collection. The new plan, available on the Land and Urban Construction Services page, states that “the architectural project needs to reserve a place to display the historical information of the old Chinese Customs House (Kwan Pou Hang Toi)”. The plans for the site – integrated into a heritage protection zone, and therefore subject to the Cultural Heritage Safeguarding Law – require a binding opinion from the Cultural Institute (IC). Remember that, in 2021, the organization decided to suspend the issuance of the PCU to investigate whether there was an Imperial Chinese Customs House there during the Qing dynasty. The decision was taken two weeks after the “green light” was given for its demolition. At the time, the then president of the IC, Mok Ian Ian, explained that the architect André Kui had found an old map relating to the site. “We studied the subject and we think that, since the academic found this ancient map, we should look at it and reevaluate”, he then added. Three years later, after the investigation, the IC said that, according to experts, the distribution of remains at numbers 5-7 of Pátio do Amparo was different from the typical arrangement found at Kwan Pou Hang Toi. Therefore, they concluded that “it is not a main structure of the former Chinese Customs House”. "The buildings that are known from the structure of this former Customs House should have been located elsewhere. The Chinese Customs House already existed before and its area was expanded", said the IC. In this context, this area of ​​the courtyard would have been “the social space for the employees' families”, which was located in the rear area of ​​the Chinese Customs House built after its expansion. Now, the new plan defines that information about Kwan Pou Hang Toi must be integrated in that location. It also restricts the maximum height of building heights to 20.5 meters, as well as the application of the 76-degree angle. The space is for housing, commercial, tourist and entertainment purposes.
Project advances in Iao Hon There are 12 more plans available for consultation until March 18th. One concerns Avenida da Longevidade, numbers 49-51, Rua Dois do Bairro do Iao Hon, 50-52, and Rua Três do Bairro do Iao Hon, 1-4, which will be used for housing. One of the plots is part of a set of seven buildings in Iao Hon that will be subject to urban renewal. The land occupies 1,549 square meters, with the proposal suggesting a maximum height of 90 meters. The maximum utilization index is 10, with a permitted usable area of ​​11,860 square meters, with the maximum number of housing units being 236. Furthermore, in response to the land requalification requirements, a request must be made to the Government to acquire an additional 300 square meters, increasing the requalified area to approximately 1,875 square meters. The area designated for planting must not be less than 35% of the land area.

item.title: Trump's Asian allies fear war with Iran will harm them
item.description:
Japanese parliamentarians, shaken by US and Israeli attacks on Iran, gathered at the ruling party headquarters in Tokyo to question authorities about evacuation plans, energy stocks and the legal basis for American action. But a question asked at the closed-door meeting, described to Reuters by a politician present, reflected a deeper fear that has haunted the corridors of power in Asia since the weekend attacks triggered chaos in the Middle East. How would the region react to a breach in its defenses if Washington diverted ships and missiles that it currently concentrates in Asia? The problem is urgent for Japan and South Korea, which are home to large American military bases that help contain the show of military force by nuclear-armed North Korea and China. “We hope this operation will be quick, limited and that resources can be promptly redirected to Asia,” said Chen Kuan-ting, a deputy from Taiwan’s ruling party. A prolonged conflict could harm “stability and peace in the Indo-Pacific”, he stressed, adding that Taipei should prepare for Beijing to intensify “coercion” while the US is distracted. Trump, who has stated that American operations in the Middle East will last four or five weeks but could last much longer, plans to meet with the Chinese President at the end of March, although Beijing has not yet confirmed his visit. The Japanese politician who reported on Monday's meeting said a senior Foreign Ministry official responded that Tokyo had sought assurances from Washington that it would not move US military assets. About 40% of U.S. Navy ships ready for operations are currently stationed in the Middle East, the Center for Strategic and International Studies said in a report last month. This includes an aircraft carrier, the Abraham Lincoln, and at least six missile destroyers based in the Pacific ports of California, Hawaii and Japan, according to the US Naval Institute. The only American aircraft carrier deployed in Asia, the George Washington, is undergoing maintenance at the base in the Japanese city of Yokosuka. “The U.S. Navy is overwhelmed,” said Bryan Clark, a former U.S. defense official specializing in naval operations at the Hudson Institute. If the war prolongs, there is a real possibility that the US will reduce its naval force in Asia to reinforce the conflict with Iran, he added, noting that “the fleet is not sufficient to maintain a constant presence in all theaters of operations”. The conflict with Iran is also depleting US ammunition reserves, which experts have been warning about for some time. The U.S. military has urged defense companies to increase production, but that could take several years. This is a concern for the US because rebuilding ammunition reserves in the Indo-Pacific helps deter China from military action against Taiwan in the medium term, said an American official, who requested anonymity because it is a sensitive issue. Japan has already faced delays in the delivery of hundreds of Tomahawk missiles ordered from the US and could be delayed further, said Jan van Tol, a senior researcher at the Center for Strategic and Budgetary Assessments.
JTM with international agencies

item.title: China opened the “Two Sessions”
item.description:
epa12793506 Chairman of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference Wang Huning (front C) delivers his speech during the opening ceremony of the Fourth Session of the 14th Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference (CPPCC) National Committee at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing, China, 04 March 2026. China's major annual political meetings, known as the 'Lianghui' or 'Two Sessions,' begin on 04 March with the opening of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference (CPPCC), while the National People's Congress (NPC) will open on 05 March. EPA/JESSICA LEE
China yesterday began its main annual political meeting with the opening of the session of the Chinese People's Political Consultative Conference (CPPCC), which precedes the inauguration, today, of the National People's Assembly (ANP), a legislative body. Chinese President Xi Jinping participated in the inaugural session at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing, accompanied by other members of the Communist Party leadership. During the meeting, the work on the agenda was approved and Wang Huning, considered one of the regime's main ideologues and close to Xi, presented the activity report of the CPPCC standing committee. The opening of the so-called “Two Sessions” coincides with the beginning of the 15th Five-Year Plan, which will define the country's economic and strategic priorities until 2030. This Thursday, the Prime Minister, Li Qiang, is expected to announce the economic growth target for 2026, which, according to analysts, could be between 4.5% and 5%. The annual meeting also takes place after the expulsion, in recent weeks, of several senior military officials from both the ANP and the CPPCC, as part of the anti-corruption campaign that has reduced the leadership of the armed forces. The Defense budget, which increased by 7.2% last year, and political messages aimed at the Armed Forces should also focus part of the attention during the debates. During the “Two Sessions”, Foreign Minister Wang Yi is expected to hold his usual annual press conference, which he often uses to anticipate the tone of Chinese diplomacy in an increasingly volatile international context, following the attacks on Iran, and before a planned visit by American President Donald Trump to China, which has not yet been officially confirmed by Beijing.
Criticism of Washington The ANP spokesperson stated yesterday that no country has the right to “control international affairs”, in reference to the recent attacks by the USA and Israel against Iran. During a press conference, Lou Qinjian stated that Iran's “sovereignty, security and territorial integrity” must be respected, also calling for the immediate interruption of military actions, and asking for a new escalation of tensions to be avoided and for dialogue to be resumed to preserve peace and stability in the Middle East. Lou stressed that mutual respect and equality between large and small countries constitute “a requirement of historical progress” and a fundamental principle of the United Nations Charter. In this sense, he stressed, no State can act “at its pleasure on the international stage”. On the other hand, he stated that “Europe needs China and China needs Europe”, defending the complementary nature of the relationship between both parties, in the face of growing trade tensions. According to him, the economic and commercial relationship between Beijing and Brussels is based on “complementarity of advantages and mutual benefit”, adding that cooperation with China contributes to European economic development and the improvement of social well-being on the continent. Lou also stated that between the two parties “there are no fundamental conflicts of interest or geopolitical contradictions” and expressed Beijing’s willingness to “adequately manage commercial differences”.
JTM with Lusa

item.title: Delta Bridge illustrates advances in Greater Bay Area connectivity
item.description: The recent records set in regional travel prove the deepening of connectivity between infrastructures and rules, as well as ties between people in the Greater Bay Area, highlighted the Xinhua agency, recalling that the Hong Kong-Zhuhai-Macao Bridge has already recorded more than 100 million passenger crossings since its inauguration in 2018. This milestone was reached shortly after the HKSAR section of the Guangzhou-Shenzhen-Hong Kong express railway line recorded the journey of its one hundred millionth passenger. So far, six major river or sea crossings have been opened to traffic in the region, forming an infrastructure network that has revitalized the integrated development of the area, serving as its main “arteries”. Today, a high-speed train takes less than 50 minutes at full speed between Guangzhou South Station and Hong Kong's West Kowloon Station, while the journey between Shenzhen's Futian and West Kowloon stations only takes about 15 minutes. “The sustained and record passenger numbers on the Hong Kong route reflect the vibrant growth driven by deepening integration between the mainland and Hong Kong,” said Jeny Yeung Mei-chun, CEO of Hong Kong MTR Corporation. The Delta Bridge has reduced travel time between Hong Kong and Zhuhai or Macau from three hours to about 45 minutes. “It took more than five years for passenger traffic at the border checkpoint to reach the first 50 million trips, but only one year and eight months for the second 50 million,” said Chen Faqiu, director of the bridge border checkpoint in Zhuhai. After more than seven years of operation, these two cross-border projects have transformed travel routines. For a growing number of people in the Greater Bay Area, taking a high-speed train to attend a cross-border meeting or crossing the bridge to shop is no longer a new experience but has now become part of everyday life. The milestone of 100 million trips is seen as concrete proof that the “one-hour circle of life” in the Greater Bay Area is becoming a reality. “These two megaprojects directly facilitate the flow of talent, capital and innovation within the Greater Bay Area, accelerating the complementary integration of urban functions, socio-economic resources and industrial chains,” said Erik Yim Kong, member of the Legislative Council of the HKSAR, as cited by Xinhua. In recent years, a series of policies have accelerated integration via the Hong Kong-Zhuhai-Macao Bridge, and authorization procedures have been optimized at major ports. “Driving from Zhuhai to Hong Kong is an incredibly smooth experience!” a Zhuhai resident surnamed Cao, who took his family to Hong Kong during the end-of-year shopping season, told the official Chinese agency. Travelers noted that procedures have been greatly simplified, eliminating previous concerns about vehicle restrictions and ferry schedules. “The two cross-border railway projects will fully integrate the Hong Kong and Shenzhen Metro networks,” said Mable Chan, Secretary for Transport and Logistics of the HKSAR Government, referring to the MTR North Link and the Hong Kong-Shenzhen West Railway Link project. Improved infrastructure and enabling policies are promoting more frequent exchanges and deeper integration between residents of Guangdong, Hong Kong and Macau. “Coming to Hengqin makes me feel very relaxed and comfortable,” said Sheeta Su, a former Macau resident who works at a university in the territory. Attracted by Hengqin's environment and increasingly convenient policies, it recently moved to the Deep Cooperation Zone. Now he commutes daily using a single-plate vehicle to Hengqin, clearing customs in no time. Since moving in at the end of 2024, Su and her family have witnessed the community's gradual development. She praises the neighborhood's environment, community activities and support facilities, including health stations and community service centers. "In the last year, more neighbors and children have moved, and many from Macau have opened stores in the nearby area. It is thriving", he assured. With her parents frequently traveling between their Guangdong hometown of Yangjiang and their home in Hengqin, Su's family represents the integrated development of the Greater Bay Area. "We explore Hengqin far and wide. Now our daily trips are expanding to cities like Zhuhai and Zhongshan," Su said, pointing out a "general trend": as distances shrink, the accelerating flow of people and capital is bringing hearts and minds closer together. "The regular flow of people naturally facilitates efficient logistics and capital circulation. Consumer and economic interactions between the north and south are continually solidifying the foundations of the Greater Bay Area's integrated development," summarized Irons Sze Wing-wai, chairman of the Federation of Beijing Organizations in Hong Kong.

item.title: New restrictions on cross-border traffic offenses
item.description:
New restrictive measures will be applied, from the 15th of this month, to vehicles that violate the rules regarding the “Macao Vehicle Circulation in Guangdong Province”, the “Regular quota for the circulation of Macau private vehicles between Macau and Hong Kong” and the Automated Parking Lot at the Hong Kong Border Post
From March 15th, restrictive measures will be applied to vehicles entering Mainland China without complying with the pre-booking rules of “Macao Vehicle Circulation in Guangdong Province”, according to the Traffic Affairs Bureau (DSAT). The new rules were published yesterday, in the Official Gazette, and also change “the provisions to be observed in the announcement regarding the regular quotas for the circulation of private vehicles in Macau between Macau and Hong Kong on the Hong Kong-Zhuhai-Macau Bridge”, says DSAT. Regarding offenses related to the movement of vehicles from Macau in Guangdong Province, the first transgression will result in the prohibition of “border crossings to enter Mainland China for one month”. For subsequent offences, each entry ban period will extend for three months. Similarly, offenses relating to pre-bookings for the “Hong Kong Border Post Automated Parking Lot” will be punished with a suspension of vehicle pre-booking eligibility for one month after the first offense and three months for subsequent offenses. Violation of the regular quota for the circulation of private vehicles in Macau between the Macao SAR and Hong Kong will be punished with one month suspension of the respective quota, in the first instance, and for three months after recurring transgressions. Furthermore, “during the period of application of the restrictive measures, all advance appointments for ‘Macao Vehicle Circulation in Guangdong Province’ and for the ‘Hong Kong Border Post Automated Parking Lot’ expire”, explains DSAT. The applicant and owner of the offending vehicle and the driver will be notified by the authorities, via SMS and alert messages in the Single Account. Those involved can also consult the relevant records on the system page “Macao Vehicle Circulation in Guangdong Province”, as well as in the “My Vehicles” tabs of the Single Account and “Company and Association Vehicles” of the “Platform for Companies and Associations”. The authorities intend to reinforce supervision, in coordination and communication with the competent regulatory authorities, and establish “a fairer usage regime”, given “the continuous increase in the number of vehicles crossing the border” under this mechanism.
P.M.

item.title: Holi Festival brings Indian artists from Hong Kong to Coloane
item.description:
The Holi Festival, also known as the Festival of Colors, brings to Macau this year close to three dozen Indian artists from the Hong Kong community. The event, which also marks International Women's Day, will take place on the terrace of Pousada de Coloane next Sunday and includes several activities. Victor Kumar, mentor of the initiative for 16 years, told TRIBUNA DE MACAU newspaper that the main objectives are to promote Indian classical dance and music and have fun in nature
VÍTOR REBELO
Live classical Indian dance, performed by around three dozen performing artists from the community of that country residing in Hong Kong, constitutes the main attraction of the Holi Festival, which will take place on Sunday, March 8, on the terrace of Pousada de Coloane, next to Cheoc Van beach. The event, also known as the Festival of Colors, will feature dancers and a singer, who will share dance and classical Indian music with members of the community living in Macau. “These artists will present a special message for International Women's Day, which is celebrated this Sunday”, the initiative's mentor told TRIBUNA DE MACAU newspaper, explaining that Women's Day has some different colors, such as purple and pink, which will be added to the usual color of the festival. Victor Kumar, an Indian residing in the Macao SAR who has promoted the festival since 2010, emphasizes that its message is “empowerment of women through classical Indian dance, to welcome the Spring season”. The Holi Festival will also feature an Indian DJ, colorful yoga sessions for girls, Bollywood dance, Brazilian dance, colored powder fight, face painting with indigenous patterns by Portuguese artist Sara Figueira and interactive games, many of which are aimed at children and families. In addition, there will be no shortage of Indian drinks and snacks. In an edition whose theme is “cultures and connections”, the organizer believes that the festival can bring people together and lead them to share joy through colors, providing fun in nature. “There will be a lot of things happening”, he says, expecting a participation of around two hundred people. Every year, the president of the Macau Indian Culture Association tries to organize the event in different locations, after having already promoted the festival in places such as Praia de Hac Sá and Galaxy. Pousada de Coloane hosts the festival of colors for the second time, after 2018. Victor Kumar recommends that people dress in white “to highlight the effect of colored powders”. The event requires the purchase of tickets, the price of which varies between 200 and 230 patacas/Hong Kong dollars, for adults, including colored powder and drink. The Hindu festival Holi originates from India, but is also celebrated in other countries, such as Suriname, Guyana, Trinidad, United Kingdom, Fiji Islands, Nepal and Bangladesh, between February and March, signifying the victory of good over evil, the arrival of Spring and the end of Winter, promoted to bring together people of different colors, races, communities, cultures and religions. Historians say that Holi precedes the birth of Christ by many centuries and there are many legends that explain its appearance.

item.title: Real estate income is expected to stabilize this year
item.description:
A market forecast from real estate consultancy JLL indicates that rents for housing and commercial spaces in Macau are expected to stabilize this year, even with asset values ​​still under pressure. In a report, the company said residential prices, which fell sharply in 2025, are expected to remain stable in 2026 following government measures to ease mortgage burdens, including stamp duty exemptions and easing loan-to-property value ratios. Banks also cut interest rates late last year, helping to sustain demand. “Property developers reduced prices to boost sales last year and, with supportive policies now in place, the residential market should stabilize in the short term,” said JLL Senior Director of Valuation and Risk in Macau, Mark Wong. “But limited long-term demand and the absence of major infrastructure projects will continue to weigh on the sector,” he added. The data shows that the equity value of housing fell by 16.5% in 2025, while that of luxury properties fell by 14.7%. Rents for conventional units fell by 10.3%, although those for high-end residences rose by 1.1%. JLL predicts that both income and asset values ​​will remain stable in 2026. The office market also faced difficulties in 2025, with general rents falling 3.4% and asset values ​​falling 7.9%. The unemployment rate rose to 15.3%, reflecting weak demand. JLL – the report also reads – expects rents to stabilize in 2026, although asset value could fall by up to 5%. The retail sector also experienced pressure, with total sales falling 5.4% between January and September last year. Rents fell by 0.9% in 2025. Although tourist areas are beginning to attract investors again, neighborhood stores remain under pressure due to high loan default rates. JLL predicts that retail asset values ​​could fall by another 5% this year. Macau's economy showed signs of resilience in 2025, with Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growing 4.7% to 415.32 billion patacas and gaming revenue rising 9.1% to 247.4 billion. Visitor arrivals grew 14.7% to 40 million, driven mainly by tourists from Mainland China. Despite the recovery in tourism and gaming, Wong warned of persistent structural challenges. “Economic growth has returned to pre-pandemic levels, but the drivers are concentrated in just a few industries,” he said. “Sentiment in the commercial market is weak and the closure of satellite casinos will continue to pose challenges,” he said.
JTM with Lusa

item.title: Illegal marriages dictated the end of around 50 BIR
item.description: In the last 10 years, around fifty Resident Identity Cards (BIR) were canceled by the Macau authorities, following 25 cases of conviction in court for the crime of false marriage. The numbers were provided to TDM by the director of Identification Services. Sónia Chan also revealed that around 280 reports of fraudulent marriages have occurred since 1999, and this is still the main way of detecting this type of crime. On the other hand, the head of the Border Control department of the Public Security Police Corps, Lao Ka Weng, indicated that, last year, 15 people and eight minors lost their right of residence due to suspicions of illegal marriages. Between 2025 and February this year, 58 cases of fake marriages and 11 of “false requests to visit family” were detected, involving a total of 139 individuals. The longest recorded duration of a sham marriage was 19 years. Lao Ka Weng stated that the associated illicit profits reach, in some situations, the amount of 120,000 patacas. “A person who commits this type of crime can be sentenced to a sentence of between two and eight years in prison”, warns the Police.

item.title: Homes without capacity for more than 1,900 elderly people
item.description:
Macau's homes are unable to accommodate more than 1,900 elderly people, according to information from the Social Action Institute. In the space of three years, there was an increase of around 36% on the waiting list. On average, people over 65 have to wait 18 months to access subsidized social facilities. The organization led by Hon Wai ensures that it has been working to respond to demand
Catarina Pereira
The number of elderly people waiting to enter Macau's homes indicates a growing trend, according to data provided to Jornal TRIBUNA DE MACAU by the Institute of Social Action (IAS). In the space of three years, between January 2023 and January 2026, the number of people aged 65 or over waiting for a place in a home rose by around 36%, from more than 1,400 to more than 1,900 elderly people. Between the end of 2024 and the beginning of this year, there was an increase of 100 people. Remember that, in 2015, around 400 elderly people were on the waiting list, according to data provided by the organization at the time. On average, elderly people have to wait a year and a half to access these subsidized social facilities, under the unified assessment and centralized transfer mechanism, said the organization led by Hon Wai. Through this mechanism, the IAS assesses residents in need and, based on the results of the assessment, organizes the waiting list and assigns them the services they need, as indicated by the president of the IAS in response to a written question from deputy José Pereira Coutinho. In the response to this newspaper, it was said that by the end of last year there were 25 nursing homes in the territory, which provide around 2,700 places. Of these homes, 15 are subsidized, offering around 2,200 places, and the remaining 10 provide a total of approximately 500 places. Since 2020, an additional 810 places have been created in homes, from 1,591 and 299, respectively. “In response to Macau's aging population, this Institute has adopted diverse measures to respond to the demand for long-term care services among the elderly,” the IAS told this newspaper. In this context, he points out, from the outset, the three homes that will be created in Zone A, providing around 1,100 beds, and which will begin operating between 2028 and 2030. The measures also include “using the construction and expansion of spaces to increase the number of places available in nursing homes”, as well as “proactively deepening community support services to respond to the care needs of the elderly, reducing the situations in which they enter nursing homes too early and, at the same time, supporting families to continue caring for the elderly, to achieve the objective of keeping the elderly at home”. In addition, the IAS also highlighted that it has maintained contact with the services in Zhuhai and Zhongshan, “in order to activate the pilot service for referral to homes in Mainland China, offering another option for elderly people who wish to seek accommodation in homes in the Greater Bay Area”. Regarding elderly people who are waiting for services in homes, the IAS refers, in its response to Pereira Coutinho, that it cooperates with social services equipment to provide them with community support services, including home care, special daytime care, support for elderly caregivers, among others. If they need the temporary accommodation service, caregivers “can request the IAS for temporary accommodation and the temporary reception service in the morning, in order to alleviate their pressure”, adds the organization. Furthermore, for elderly people who have “urgent needs” for accommodation and who have financial difficulties, “the IAS will provide economic support, in order to support them in admission to private homes, allowing them to receive appropriate care in a timely manner during the waiting period for home services”. In parallel, Hon Wai says that the study on the use of resources will continue, “in the sense that on Government land intended for public housing and future new urban areas, etc., spaces can be reserved to increase the supply of different types of support services for the elderly”. Community support services “will also be increased and deepened” to “respond to the care needs of elderly people who spend their old age at home and in the community”.
Lar da Misericórdia welcomes 110 elderly people The Lar da Nossa Senhora da Misericórdia currently houses 110 elderly people, Santa Casa told the TRIBUNA DE MACAU newspaper, also clarifying that, since July last year, the vacancies have entered the central system for allocating vacancies. “That is, the Social Action Institute started to manage a large part of our vacancies”, explained the institution’s general secretary, Gisela Nunes. As noted, the waiting list is high, compared to the available places.

item.title: Orchestral music program aims to create “talent reserve”
item.description:
澳門演藝學院迎春音樂會2026
This year, a training program will be launched for young local musicians who study instruments related to symphony orchestras or traditional Chinese music, said Leong Wai Man. The initiative, organized by the Macau Orchestra Society, will have a dozen places and will serve to establish “a reserve of artistic talents”, revealed the vice-president of the Advisory Council for Cultural Development. Furthermore, in April, there will be a new edition of the “National Reading Week”
PEDRO MILHEIRÃO The Macau Orchestra Society will launch a training program this year aimed at young local musicians, who study instruments related to symphony orchestras or traditional Chinese music, Leong Wai Man announced yesterday. At a press conference, the vice-president of the Advisory Council for Cultural Development explained that the program aims to “increase opportunities for successful employment in professional orchestras, for local musical talents”, and contribute “to the construction of a reserve of artistic talents in Macau”. “In this program, the objective is to allow students who are studying instruments and music to have opportunities to deepen their training and career planning,” said the president of the Cultural Institute (IC). "If they intend to participate in orchestras, there will certainly be opportunities to compete or to test themselves in other orchestras. We know that for any orchestra in Macau, or elsewhere, tests are necessary. Through this type of training, we intend to increase their skills so that they can have more advantages in the future and a better professional career", he stressed. Referring to the Macau Orchestra and the Macau Chinese Orchestra, Leong stated that "the trainees will learn with the two existing orchestras and, if possible, participate in concerts or shows in the communities, which we organize annually. There will also be classes with masters. [The trainees] will be included in our orchestras to learn, and to absorb knowledge, which will certainly help them in the future", said Leong Wai Man. The person in charge projected that the training will be aimed at “a dozen trainees”, since “if there are too many there will be no helping hands”, noting that the project is still in the planning phase. “We want to have a very good program so that young people who are studying music can, after this training, have more opportunities for employment or even for planning their professional career”, reinforced Ieng Weng Fat, member of the Council. Ieng also highlighted the desire to improve “local training in terms of artistic talents”.
“Reading Week” with new name In April, the “National Reading Week” will also take place, a new name for the initiative that the IC has been carrying out in recent years. The activities will be organized between the IC, the Directorate of Education and Youth Development Services, the Institute of Social Action and civil organizations, “with the aim of creating a reading environment for everyone” and “deepening the construction of a reading city”. Miguel de Senna Fernandes, who participated in the Advisory Council meeting, told this newspaper that the initiative “could be a good incentive”. "It was important for this to happen, in order to create conditions for the use of reading to increase. Portuguese schools themselves have also greatly encouraged this aspect", he noted. In this context, the member of the Council and president of the Association of Macanese also mentioned that “it would be good if reading was not only in the Chinese language, but also to promote reading in the Portuguese language”, which would represent “a very good sign”.
International Parade once again features Portuguese groups The vice-president of the Advisory Council for Cultural Development said that the International Parade, scheduled for the 29th of this month, will be attended by Portuguese groups. In the future, “we will have a more detailed presentation on the organization and organization of transport,” added Leong Wai Man, without providing further details. For Miguel de Senna Fernandes, member of the Council, the participation of Portuguese groups is “always welcome”, having also stressed the importance of “never forgetting that there is a Portuguese side to take into account”. “The presence of Portuguese groups in Macau has been recurrent, in all cultural activities”, he said, highlighting that “the president has done a great job of promoting this aspect, so it is to be praised”.

item.title: ITTF World Cup champions defend titles in Macau
item.description:
Macau will once again welcome the international table tennis elite for the third consecutive year of the World Cup. The competition will take place at the Galaxy Arena between March 30th and April 5th. The presence of the champions of last year's edition has been confirmed, Brazilian Hugo Calderano and Chinese Sun Yingsha, among other figures who occupy the top places in the ranking
VÍTOR REBELO
The international table tennis elite returns in force to Macau for another edition of the World Cup, which will take place at the Galaxy Arena for the third consecutive time. Among the players already confirmed, the winners of the 2025 competition are the Brazilian Hugo Calderano, in men's, and the Chinese Sun Yingsha, in women's. The Brazilian representative was the big sensation in last year's tournament, beating the main candidate, Lin Shidong, from China, in the final. According to the current ranking, Calderano is fourth and Shidong third. The leadership belongs to Chinese Wang Chuqin, ahead of Swedish Truls Moregard, players who will also be present at the tournament organized by the International Table Tennis Federation (ITTF). As in previous editions, the Chinese delegation will include big names. In addition to those already mentioned, Liang Jingkun, Zhou Qihao and Wen Ruibo will also come. In the women's sector, Sun Yingsha leads the ranking, ahead of compatriots Wang Manyu and Chen Xingtong. Kuai Man and Wang Yidi will also participate in the race. During yesterday's press conference, the organization also assured that the under-19 champions in the men's and women's singles category of the ITTF World Youth Championships, Kawakami Ryuusei, from Japan, and Qin Yuxuan, from China, will perform, in addition to players qualified through competitions held on five continents. The final places, in both draws, are determined through the positions in the world ranking, so the participation of Zhu Yuling, from Macau, aged 31, who currently occupies fourth position, should be assured. As has happened in previous tournaments held in the territory, the event will once again feature 48 players, who will compete in the Evans Cup in the men's matches and the Hammarlund Cup in the women's matches. These trophies have legendary histories dating back to 1980 and 1996 respectively. The event, organized by the Sports Institute (ID), with support from Galaxy Entertainment Group (GEG), World Table Tennis Management Group Limited and Macau General Ping-Pong Association, will be held in two phases. In the first, players will be divided into 16 groups of three, where they will play matches in a “round robin” system (all-against-all). The four best will be distributed across Groups 1 to 4. Those who secure first place in each series will then move on to the knockout competition. The Phase 1 group tests, from March 30th to April 1st, will have two sessions per day, the first starting at 9:30 am and the second at 5:00 pm. For the qualifiers (round of 16 on the 2nd and 3rd of April and quarter-finals on the 4th of April), the first session will start at 11:00 am and the second at 6:00 pm. The semi-finals and final will be held on Sunday, April 5th, at 11am and 7:30pm respectively. In presenting the tournament, the president of ID, Lei Si Leng, highlighted the “success” of previous editions and said that “the synergistic effects of these major sporting events have been boosting the development of Macau’s sports sector, as well as the growth of sports tourism and other associated sectors”. In turn, the director of GEG, Philip Cheng, stated that, in this year, which marks the ITTF's centenary, the company will continue to collaborate with other organizing entities to promote a series of parallel events and will establish partnerships with local social service organizations to hold table tennis events, “in order to support the high-quality development of Macau's sports sector”. In the first round, tickets will go on sale from 12pm this Friday. Meanwhile, an Official Hospitality Experience will be launched, providing table tennis enthusiasts with special show experiences, available for purchase via the Damai app and mini-program. Spectators will be able to enjoy exclusive privileges including prime seats to watch the games, hospitality lounges and souvenirs.

item.title: Paris week begins with ‘consolidation’ of styles
item.description:
epa12791654 A model presents a creation from the Fall/Winter 2026 collection by Demoo fashion house during the Paris Fashion Week, in Paris, France, 03 March 2026. The presentation of the women's collections runs from 02 to 10 March 2026. EPA/CHRISTOPHE PETIT TESSON
The Paris Women's Autumn/Winter Fashion Week began this week, an edition of “consolidation” of new designers at the helm of major brands, such as the Spanish Loewe, in an international context of crisis in the Middle East. The war triggered by US and Israeli attacks against Iran, however, did not change the event's schedule. There will be “neither cancellation nor change”, Pascal Morand, executive president of the Federation of Haute Couture and Fashion, told AFP. The Week officially opened with the parade of students from the French Fashion Institute, one of the most prestigious training centers in the world. Among around 50 looks presented, a dozen were seen with padding, padding and real XXL airbags to cushion impacts, as if reflecting young people's concern about protecting themselves from the world. Among the names that paraded, several young brands stood out, such as the French Weinsanto and the Belgian Julie Kegels. In its second time on the official program, the latter presented a collection with many layered pieces, which gave a geometric image to the whole. Other silhouettes were more fluid, with capes and double fabrics on the back that resembled sails in motion. Colorful high-top leather boots harmonized with the masks worn by the models. In a context of economic difficulties in the luxury sector, fashion heavyweights have changed their creative director in recent years, such as Dior and Chanel. At the previous presentation of the women's collections for Spring-Summer 2026, at least 10 designers made their debut, including Matthieu Blazy (Chanel), Jonathan Anderson (Dior) and Pierpaolo Piccioli (Balenciaga). "We witnessed a formidable creative impulse in September, driven by the simultaneous renewal of several artistic directions. (…) This edition is part of a period of consolidation, with fewer surprise effects", said Morand about Paris Fashion Week, which will have around 70 shows until March 10th. Among the most anticipated shows are those of Dior and Chanel, next Monday. Although its creatives, Anderson and Blazy, respectively, have already made their debut in these major houses, both for the women's and haute couture collections, their proposals will once again focus attention. These designers “will have to consolidate what they have started to define, and as their message so far has not been entirely clear, it will be interesting to see how they position themselves this time,” said Jeanne Le Bault, chief fashion editor at Marie-Claire magazine. This is also the case with the Spanish company Loewe, currently part of the French group LVMH. Its new artistic directors, Americans Jack McCollough and Lazaro Hernandez, debuted at the helm of the brand in October, after the departure of Jonathan Anderson, who for a decade rejuvenated the image of the historic house. At that show, McCollough and Hernandez, founders of New York's Proenza Schouler in 2002, opted for an explosion of colors, and some proposals directly referred to the multicolored aesthetic of filmmaker Pedro Almodóvar. After being absent from the men's edition at the beginning of the year, the American duo's new collection will focus all eyes on Friday. Another highly anticipated show is the debut of Antonin Tron at Balmain. After working for Givenchy, Louis Vuitton and Balenciaga, the French designer will have the difficult challenge of succeeding the iconic Olivier Rousteing. Uruguayan Gabriela Hearst will also take her new collection to Paris. This designer's creations are based on the concept of sustainable luxury and, often, the fabrics are made with materials left over from previous seasons.
JTM with international agencies
